candidate.subject: esphome: verify real polling and reconnect

acceptance.isolation: private Linux user, mount, and network namespaces
acceptance.transport: Noise with a public test-only key
assertions: both original MCL SHA-256 values match before execution
a real MGMT process converges esphome0.mcl and esphome-blink.mcl without source changes
esphome0 resolves legacy object IDs and corrects switch and number state
esphome-blink resolves current entity names, streams a device log, and corrects switch state
polling uses two encrypted connections and has zero active connections between cycles
a queued number command wakes polling and reaches the second encrypted cycle
MGMT reconnects after an encrypted persistent connection drops
MGMT records a positive completed outage
reconnect does not replay an unrequested command
ten consecutive race-enabled real-driver polling and reconnect runs pass
the unchanged conveyor MCL remains green

defect_fixed.summary: a stale Configure wake could truncate the polling snapshot-settle window
defect_fixed.resolution: drain a queued wake before a connection cycle and restart only when the configuration generation changed

limitations: No physical device was flashed or actuated.
The baseline scenarios prove initial snapshots and corrective commands; a later pushed-state timeline remains pending.
Text-sensor runtime behavior is not exercised by these MCL files.
A simulator is not ESPHome firmware, so hardware evidence remains pending.
